# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python data processing application. Start from a full Ubuntu base image and install Python, pip, curl, wget, git, vim, and other useful development tools. This gives us flexibility for debugging and running various scripts in production.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest  # start with the latest version of a Ubuntu base image on dockerhub or you can use any specific tag here if present like 'ubuntu-18.04' etc...
MAINTAINER your_email@example.com "Your name"   # This will set up who owns and how to reach about this Docker container (optional) – the default is not to mention it, which means no contact in such cases - https://www.docker.com/integrations-contact
RUN apt update && \     // updates packages lists for installing prerequisites while installation will run as part of install step below and then we can proceed with adding dependencies using pip or other method here instead the first line – "apt" is a package manager in Ubuntu used to handle dependency management, but it's not necessary if your Dockerfile doesn’t contain any explicit command for installing packages.
RUN apt-get install -y python3 curl wget git vim netcat # Install required tools inside the container running 'ubuntu:latest'.  // "apt" in ubuntu is similar to a package manager on Linux, and it's used here as per your requirement only which allows us flexibility for debugging.
RUN pip3 install --upgrade pip     # Upgrades Pip with respecting system-wide python versions setup during the first run of Dockerfile - “pip” in Ubuntu is Python’s package manager but if not installed by default then it will be automatically handled at docker build time, using 'python' as an equivalent to install packages and dependencies.
RUN curl https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py | python3   # Download pip from bootstrap pypi server for Python 2 & downgrading the version of PIP in a secure way (used with --user option). // "curl" is used to download file, '|' - pipe operation here means it’s transferring data as per command line.
RUN git --version && useradd -rm $USER   # Checks if Git installed or not and creates new system account named after logged-in users for easy access in case they need permission from the server side with proper permissions setup, to avoid any confusion due SSH login problem that could happen when we are using default Ubuntu OS.
RUN curl -sSL https://get.docker.com/ | sh   # Installs Docker CE on ubuntu (it will also handle upgrading and removing old versions of docker). // "curl" is used for fetching file from the internet, '|' to pipe in a script created by another command ('sh'), which allows us as user executing this line inside our container.
RUN echo "alias ll='ls -l'" >> ~/.bashrc   # Adds an alias (in bash) so we can use `ll` instead of just using 'ls'. // This is a common way to make shell script commands more powerful by providing additional functionality for users who run the container.
RUN echo "alias la='ls -a'" >> ~/.bashrc   # Similar as above but shows all hidden files (the beginning with '.') – these are often used in development environments when not interested about sensitive or 'secret information'. // This is a common way to make shell script commands more powerful by providing additional functionality for users who run the container.
RUN echo "alias ll='ls -l'" >> ~/.bashrc   # Adds an alias (in bash) so we can use `ll` instead of just using 'ls'. // This is a common way to make shell script commands more powerful by providing additional functionality for users who run the container.
RUN git config --global user.email "your_name@example.com"   # Sets your global username used in GitHub (optional). If no name given it will default as 'Your Name' or return error, because `git` does not allow you to setup a login email on first run using the Dockerfile method only if there is other way than this command below which would be more proper.
RUN git config --global user.name "your_email@example.com"   # Sets your global username used in GitHub (optional). If no name given it will default as 'Your Name' or return error, because `git` does not allow you to setup a login email on first run using the Dockerfile method only if there is other way than this command below which would be more proper.
RUN pip3 install jupyter && \   # Installs Jupyter Notebook with respecting system-wide Python versions (installs in your working directory). // "jupyter" installed here to serve as a note taking and experiment environment for data science, similar functionality can be achieved through different ways.
